tech_lead_synthesis.task_description: Create comprehensive unit tests for src/lib/concerts.ts to achieve 80% code coverage
tech_lead_synthesis.summary: Implement Vitest-based unit testing for src/lib/concerts.ts by reusing existing Vitest configuration from the feature/unit-testing-framework branch (commit 7507a32). Create a focused test suite covering 25+ exported functions with 80% coverage, using mocked Prisma client and GDPR-compliant anonymized test data. Security-critical authorization tests and GDPR privacy flag tests are mandatory for merge approval.

high_agreement: Use Vitest as the testing framework (5/5 agents agree)
Reuse existing vitest.config.ts and vitest.setup.ts from feature/unit-testing-framework branch (Architect, Pragmatist, QE agree)
Mock Prisma client globally via vi.mock('@/lib/prisma') (5/5 agents agree)
Create single focused test file src/lib/concerts.test.ts (Architect, Pragmatist, QE agree)
Use anonymized GDPR-compliant test data with @test.example.com emails (QE, Security, Architect agree)
Add test scripts to package.json: test, test:watch, test:coverage (5/5 agents agree)
Target 80% line + branch coverage (5/5 agents agree)
Group tests by functional area: CRUD, pagination, filters, statistics, spending (Architect, Pragmatist, QE agree)
moderate_agreement: Create separate test-fixtures.ts for mock data factories (Architect proposes, Pragmatist suggests inline, QE neutral)
Add TESTING.md documentation (QE mandates, Architect supports, Pragmatist defers)
Test private functions like parseSupportingActIds (Architect tests, Pragmatist skips)
Mock geocoding external dependencies (Resilience mandates, Pragmatist suggests simple mock)

conflicts_resolved.topic: Test organization: separate fixture files vs inline mocks
conflicts_resolved.architect_said: Create __tests__/lib/test-fixtures.ts and __tests__/lib/prisma-mock.ts for reusable mock factories
conflicts_resolved.pragmatist_said: Keep mock data inline in concerts.test.ts. No separate utilities until needed.
conflicts_resolved.resolution: Inline mocks in concerts.test.ts — Pragmatist wins
conflicts_resolved.reasoning: This is the first test file. YAGNI principle applies. If a second test file needs similar mocks, then extract. The Architect concedes this is speculative abstraction.

conflicts_resolved.architect_said: Test parseSupportingActIds and bandToTransformed explicitly (exported for testing)
conflicts_resolved.pragmatist_said: Skip private functions; they're tested implicitly through public API
conflicts_resolved.resolution: Test only exported functions — Pragmatist wins
conflicts_resolved.reasoning: parseSupportingActIds and bandToTransformed are NOT exported in concerts.ts. Testing them would require exporting or file manipulation. Implicit coverage through CRUD tests is sufficient for 80% target.

conflicts_resolved.resilience_said: Add tests for geocoding failures, Prisma transaction deadlocks, query timeouts
conflicts_resolved.quality_engineer_said: Focus on CRUD, pagination, statistics per ACs. Error handling is follow-up work.
conflicts_resolved.pragmatist_said: Out of scope. Unit tests assume mocks succeed. Integration tests handle failures.
conflicts_resolved.resolution: Document error handling gaps as tech debt; focus on core 80% coverage — QE + Pragmatist win
conflicts_resolved.reasoning: ACs don't mention error handling tests. Resilience gaps are valid but not blocking for this story. Document in TESTING.md as 'Known Limitations' for future work.

conflicts_resolved.security_said: CRITICAL: Add tests for updateConcert/deleteConcert non-owner scenarios. Tests must FAIL if authorization removed.
conflicts_resolved.pragmatist_said: Authorization is in API routes (session check), not concerts.ts. Concerts.ts assumes userId is valid.
conflicts_resolved.quality_engineer_said: Security is right — ownership verification IS in concerts.ts (lines 901-906, 1076-1080). Must test.
conflicts_resolved.resolution: Add authorization tests — Security + QE win (CRITICAL priority)
conflicts_resolved.reasoning: concerts.ts DOES verify userId matches before update/delete. This is security-critical. Pragmatist concedes after QE shows line references. Security tests are mandatory for merge.

conflicts_resolved.quality_engineer_said: 5 explicit GDPR tests with gdpr_flag=true metadata: hideLocationPublic, hideCostPublic, isPublic
conflicts_resolved.pragmatist_said: These flags are checked at API/component level, not in concerts.ts. Out of scope.
conflicts_resolved.security_said: Filtering responsibility must be documented in JSDoc. Tests assume caller applies flags.
conflicts_resolved.resolution: Document filtering responsibility in JSDoc; add 2 basic privacy tests as examples — Security compromise
conflicts_resolved.reasoning: Privacy flags ARE NOT enforced in concerts.ts (it returns raw data). Responsibility is at API route level. However, QE's point about test data anonymization is valid. Compromise: Add JSDoc clarifying caller responsibility + 2 demo tests showing how to verify response fields.

files.description: Add Vitest devDependencies (vitest, @vitest/ui, @vitest/coverage-v8, jsdom) and test scripts (test, test:watch, test:coverage). Copy exact versions from feature/unit-testing-framework branch (commit 7507a32).
Vitest configuration with TypeScript support, jsdom environment, setupFiles reference, coverage thresholds (80% lines, 80% branches, 80% functions), @vitest/ui reporter, path aliases (@/ for src).

Global test setup: Mock Prisma client via vi.mock('@/lib/prisma'), mock Next.js components (next/image, next/link), mock external utilities (@/utils/data for getGeocodingData, @/utils/helpers for cityToSlug). Set up test environment variables.

Comprehensive unit test suite for all 25+ exported functions in concerts.ts. Organized by functional area: Utilities (getStartOfToday), CRUD (create/update/delete with authorization tests), Queries (getBy*, getAll*), Pagination (getConcertsPaginated with cursor logic), Filtering, Statistics (caching), Spending (aggregation), Edge cases (duplicate detection, null handling). Uses vi.mocked(prisma) for mocking, anonymized GDPR-compliant test data, JSDoc documenting privacy flag responsibility.

key_functions.name: test_createConcert_with_existing_match_links_user
key_functions.description: Verifies deduplication logic when concert matches existing by date/location/headliner

key_functions.name: test_updateConcert_non_owner_returns_null (CRITICAL)
key_functions.description: Security test: Verifies non-owner cannot update concert. Test MUST FAIL if authorization check removed.

key_functions.name: test_deleteConcert_non_owner_returns_false (CRITICAL)
key_functions.description: Security test: Verifies non-owner cannot delete concert. Test MUST FAIL if authorization check removed.

key_functions.name: test_getConcertsPaginated_forward_cursor_pagination
key_functions.description: Verifies cursor-based pagination with forward direction (date ASC, id ASC)

key_functions.name: test_getConcertsPaginated_backward_cursor_pagination
key_functions.description: Verifies cursor-based pagination with backward direction (date DESC, id DESC)

key_functions.name: test_getConcertsPaginated_with_bandSlug_filter
key_functions.description: Verifies bandSlug filter applies to headliner

key_functions.name: test_getConcertStatistics_uses_cache
key_functions.description: Verifies unstable_cache wrapper is used for statistics

key_functions.name: test_findMatchingConcert_within_tolerance
key_functions.description: Verifies coordinate tolerance (0.001 degrees) for matching

files.description: Testing guidelines and patterns. Sections: Vitest Setup, Running Tests, Mocking Patterns (Prisma, External APIs), GDPR Compliance (test data anonymization, privacy flags), concerts.ts Test Patterns (authorization, pagination, statistics), Known Limitations (error handling, resilience gaps).
Add reference to TESTING.md in Commands section: 'See TESTING.md for writing tests, mocking patterns, and GDPR compliance guidelines'.

implementation_order: 1. Copy vitest.config.ts and vitest.setup.ts from feature/unit-testing-framework branch (commit 7507a32)
2. Update package.json with Vitest devDependencies and test scripts
3. Extend vitest.setup.ts to mock Prisma client and external utilities
4. Write src/lib/concerts.test.ts with ~25-35 test cases covering all 25+ functions
5. Run yarn test:coverage to verify 80% coverage threshold
6. Write TESTING.md with patterns and GDPR compliance guidelines

critical_items_addressed: GDPR: Test data uses anonymized PII (test-user-@test.example.com, mock UUIDs)
Security: Authorization tests for updateConcert/deleteConcert non-owner scenarios (MUST FAIL if checks removed)
Testing: Comprehensive coverage of 25+ exported functions targeting 80%
Testing: Prisma client mocked globally, no real database access
Security: JSDoc documenting privacy flag filtering responsibility
high_items_addressed: Testing: CRUD operations tested (create with deduplication, update with fork logic, delete)
Testing: Edge cases tested (duplicate detection, null handling, coordinate tolerance)
Naming: Test names follow test_{function}_{condition}_{expected} pattern
unaddressed_items: Resilience: Geocoding failure handling (not in scope — concerts.ts doesn't handle failures, API routes do)
Resilience: Prisma transaction deadlock retry logic (not in scope — unit tests mock successful transactions)
Resilience: Database query timeouts (not in scope — unit tests don't use real DB)
Observability: No logging in concerts.ts (tech debt documented in TESTING.md Known Limitations)

context7_lookups_required.query: mocking Prisma client with TypeScript, vi.mock() patterns, beforeEach setup
watch mode and coverage UI configuration

risks.description: Feature branch divergence: vitest.config.ts/vitest.setup.ts on feature/unit-testing-framework may have diverged from current base branch
risks.mitigation: Pull exact files from commit 7507a32 to ensure reproducibility. Verify Vitest versions match.

risks.description: Prisma mock complexity: Raw SQL queries ($queryRaw) in statistics functions may be difficult to mock accurately
risks.mitigation: Use vi.spyOn(prisma, '$queryRaw') to verify query structure rather than simulating SQL logic. Acceptable for 80% coverage target.

risks.description: Authorization test brittleness: Tests for non-owner scenarios could pass even if authorization is broken if mock data is wrong
risks.mitigation: Write tests that explicitly set non-matching userId and ASSERT that update/delete returns null/false. Add comments explaining test must FAIL if authorization removed.

risks.description: GDPR compliance: Test data with production PII could leak in CI logs
risks.mitigation: Code review checklist: Verify all test fixtures use @test.example.com emails, mock UUIDs, generic artist names. NO production data.

risks.description: Coverage gaming: 80% coverage could be achieved by testing happy paths only, missing error branches
risks.mitigation: Include edge cases: null handling, empty arrays, invalid cursors, coordinate tolerance boundaries. QE to review coverage report for untested branches.

architect.notes: File structure adopted (vitest.config.ts, vitest.setup.ts, concerts.test.ts, TESTING.md). Test organization adopted (by functional area). Rejected: separate test-fixtures.ts (YAGNI), prisma-mock.ts (inline mocks), testing private functions (not exported).

quality-engineer.notes: All test requirements adopted: CRUD, pagination, statistics, edge cases. GDPR compliance enforced (anonymized data). Authorization tests accepted as CRITICAL. Rejected: 5 GDPR-specific tests reduced to 2 demo tests + JSDoc (filtering not in concerts.ts).

pragmatist.notes: Scope assessment (MEDIUM) adopted. Reuse from feature/unit-testing-framework branch adopted. Inline mocks adopted (no separate utilities). Testing private functions skipped (adopted). Complexity warnings addressed (fork logic, pagination, $queryRaw).

resilience-performance.notes: Geocoding mock requirement adopted (external dependency). Rejected: error handling tests (out of scope), transaction deadlock tests (unit tests don't use real DB), query timeout tests (mocks succeed), retry logic (not in concerts.ts), idempotency improvements (follow-up work). Gaps documented as Known Limitations in TESTING.md.

security-specialist.notes: Authorization tests adopted (CRITICAL priority). Test data anonymization adopted (GDPR compliance). JSDoc privacy flag documentation adopted. Cursor validation tests adopted. Rejected: SQL injection tests for cursor parameter (Prisma parameterizes), unbounded array deserialization tests (parseSupportingActIds not exported).
